chapter_context.summary: Details Pashhur the priest beating and putting Jeremiah in stocks, followed by Jeremiah's passionate complaint and cursing the day of his birth.

outline.text: Pashhur the Priest Beating Jeremiah and Placing in Stocks
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Pashhur the Priest Beating Jeremiah and Placing in Stocks (20:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Jeremiah's Soul Cry: 'His word was as a burning fire'
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jeremiah's Soul Cry: 'His word was as a burning fire' (20:7-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Jeremiah Cursing the Day of His Birth
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jeremiah Cursing the Day of His Birth (20:14-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Warning
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of warning, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 20, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
